rationale: A precise strike on Tethys’ border command posts will shatter their defensive cohesion and signal irreversible escalation.
Deploying our Black Fleet to the Azure Strait pressures Tethys’ offshore energy infrastructure and cuts covert supply routes.
Conducting live-fire drills near the border reinforces domestic morale and deters internal hesitation.
Covertly disrupting Tethys’ rail logistics network will delay reinforcements without triggering full-scale retaliation.
Infiltrating and degrading their air defense systems creates exploitable gaps for future operations.
Orchestrating a staged attack on a neutral pipeline station could isolate Tethys diplomatically and justify broader action.
Accelerating reserves of critical minerals and grain insulates us from Tethys-led supply shocks during prolonged crisis.
Locking in emergency supply deals now prevents economic strangulation if Tethys closes its pipelines.
Quietly shorting Tethys’ sovereign bonds undermines market confidence and reduces their war financing capacity.
Deter immediate incursion without triggering escalation by demonstrating readiness and situational awareness.
Signal defensive cohesion and boost domestic morale through highly visible, non-provocative drills near interior regions.
Exploit stretched Novaris logistics with precision, deniable strikes to delay offensive buildup.
Disrupt energy exports flowing through Tethys to undermine Novaris’ economic stability and war funding.
Degrade coordination of spring offensive by targeting logistics and communication nodes.
Sow internal distrust in Novaris military leadership by framing corrupt procurement practices to delay deployments.
Solidify international legal support by forming a defensive alliance that increases Novaris’ political cost of aggression.
Explore conditional de-escalation to buy time while strengthening defenses, even if low chance of success.
Position ourselves as responsible actors to win global sympathy and isolate Novaris diplomatically.
Secure critical energy and food reserves to extend survival beyond six months and deter economic coercion.
Lock in emergency supply routes and alternative markets to offset potential Novaris-led blockades.
Gradually devalue domestic currency to boost export competitiveness and fund war economy discreetly.
